---
title: "The Impact of Cursor Adoption on Velocity and Quality - Borusyak et al. (2021) DiD Approach"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(dev = 'CairoPNG', dpi = 150)
options(bitmapType = 'cairo')
utils::globalVariables(c(
    "estimate", "is_significant", "conf.low", "conf.high",
    "term", "setting", "outcome", "outcome_label", "time"
))
```

## Loading Required Packages

```{r load_packages}
library(didimputation)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)
library(modelsummary)
library(fixest)
library(gridExtra)
library(Cairo)
```

## Loading and Preparing the Data

```{r load_data}
panel_data <- fread("../data/panel_event_monthly.csv")

# Load repo metrics data for cursor adoption percentages
repo_metrics <- fread("../data/repo_metrics.csv")
repos_data <- fread("../data/repos.csv")
matching_data <- fread("../data/matching.csv")
lang_map <- setNames(repos_data$repo_primary_language, repos_data$repo_name)

# Build treatment-to-control mapping from matching data
treatment_repos <- matching_data[matching_data$group == "treatment", ]
get_matched_controls <- function(treat_repo_names) {
    matched <- treatment_repos[treatment_repos$repo_name %in% treat_repo_names, ]
    controls <- unique(c(matched$matched_control_1, matched$matched_control_2, matched$matched_control_3))
    controls[!is.na(controls) & controls != ""]
}

# Convert variables to DiD required format
# Keep original repo names (strings) for mapping
panel_data$repo_name_str <- panel_data$repo_name
panel_data$repo_name <- as.numeric(factor(panel_data$repo_name))

panel_data$primary_language <- lang_map[panel_data$repo_name_str]
panel_data$lang_group <- case_when(
    panel_data$primary_language %in% c("JavaScript", "TypeScript") ~ "JS/TS",
    panel_data$primary_language == "Python" ~ "Python",
    panel_data$primary_language == "Go" ~ "Go",
    panel_data$primary_language == "Rust" ~ "Rust",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
)

panel_data$time <- as.numeric(gsub("-", "", panel_data$time))
panel_data$event[!is.na(panel_data$event)] <- as.numeric(
    gsub("-", "", panel_data$event[!is.na(panel_data$event)]))
panel_data$event[is.na(panel_data$event)] <- 0
panel_data <- panel_data[panel_data$event < 202401 # never treated
        | (panel_data$event > 202407 & panel_data$event <= 202503)]
panel_data$event <- as.integer(panel_data$event)
panel_data$quality_warnings <- panel_data$code_smells + panel_data$vulnerabilities + panel_data$bugs

# Log transform skewed variables
panel_data$commits <- log(panel_data$commits + 1)
panel_data$lines_added <- log(panel_data$lines_added + 1)
panel_data$quality_warnings <- log(panel_data$quality_warnings + 1)
panel_data$cognitive_complexity <- log(panel_data$cognitive_complexity + 1)
panel_data$duplicated_lines_density <- log(panel_data$duplicated_lines_density + 1)
panel_data$comment_lines_density <- log(panel_data$comment_lines_density + 1)
panel_data$age <- log(panel_data$age + 1)
panel_data$contributors <- log(panel_data$contributors + 1)
panel_data$stars <- log(panel_data$stars + 1)
panel_data$issues <- log(panel_data$issues + 1)
panel_data$issue_comments <- log(panel_data$issue_comments + 1)

# Create treatment indicator and time since treatment variables
panel_data$treated <- ifelse(panel_data$event > 0, 1, 0)
panel_data$time_since_treatment <- ifelse(panel_data$treated == 1,
                                         panel_data$time - panel_data$event,
                                         NA)

# Build simple mapping: repo_name -> cursor adoption %
adoption_pct <- setNames(repo_metrics$cursor_adoptor_percentage, repo_metrics$repo_name)

glimpse(panel_data)
```

## DiD Analysis with DiD Imputation Package

```{r did_analysis}
# List of outcome variables
outcome_vars <- c(
    "commits",
    "lines_added",
    "quality_warnings",
    # "comment_lines_density",
    "duplicated_lines_density",
    "cognitive_complexity"
)
outcome_names <- c(
    "Commits",
    "Lines Added",
    "Static Analysis Warnings",
    # "Comment Lines Density",
    "Duplicated Lines Density",
    "Code Complexity"
)
```

## Aggregate Treatment Effects

```{r aggregate_effects}
# Function to extract static treatment effects (overall effect)
get_static_effects <- function(outcome_var, data) {
    # Run static DiD (no horizon/pretrends specified)
    static_result <- did_imputation(
        data = data,
        yname = outcome_var,
        gname = "event",
        tname = "time",
        idname = "repo_name",
        first_stage = ~ age + ncloc + contributors + stars + issues | repo_name + time
    )

    return(static_result)
}

# Calculate static effects for each outcome
static_results <- list()
for (var in outcome_vars) {
    static_results[[var]] <- get_static_effects(var, panel_data)
}

# Create a dataframe with all static effects
static_df <- data.frame(
    Outcome = names(static_results),
    Estimate = sapply(static_results, function(x) x$estimate[x$term == "treat"]),
    SE = sapply(static_results, function(x) x$std.error[x$term == "treat"]),
    CI_Lower = sapply(static_results, function(x) x$conf.low[x$term == "treat"]),
    CI_Upper = sapply(static_results, function(x) x$conf.high[x$term == "treat"])
)

# Clean outcome names for display
static_df$Outcome <- gsub("log\\((.+) \\+ 1\\)", "\\1", static_df$Outcome)
static_df$Outcome <- gsub("_", " ", static_df$Outcome)
static_df$Outcome <- tools::toTitleCase(static_df$Outcome)

# Display static effects
knitr::kable(static_df,
    digits = 3,
    caption = "Static Treatment Effects of Cursor Adoption (DiD Imputation)"
)
```

## Dynamic Treatment Effects (Event Study)

```{r dynamic_analysis}
# Function to run dynamic analysis for a given outcome
run_dynamic_analysis <- function(outcome_var, data) {
    # Run didimputation with horizon parameter for -6 to 6 months
    dynamic_result <- did_imputation(
        data = data,
        yname = outcome_var,
        gname = "event",
        tname = "time",
        idname = "repo_name",
        first_stage = ~ age + ncloc + contributors + stars + issues | repo_name + time,
        horizon = -6:6,
        pretrends = -6:-2
    )

    return(dynamic_result)
}

# Run dynamic analysis for each outcome
dynamic_results <- list()
for (var in outcome_vars) {
    cat("Running dynamic analysis for:", var, "\n")
    dynamic_results[[var]] <- run_dynamic_analysis(var, panel_data)
}
```

```{r event_study, fig.width=11, fig.height=2.5}
# Prepare data for all outcomes in a single faceted plot
event_study_data <- list()
for (i in seq_along(outcome_vars)) {
    if (outcome_vars[i] %in% names(dynamic_results)) {
        did_result <- dynamic_results[[outcome_vars[i]]]
        # Filter for dynamic effects (exclude static "treat" term only)
        dynamic_data <- did_result[did_result$term != "treat", ]

        if (nrow(dynamic_data) > 0) {
            # Create a data frame for this outcome
            df <- data.frame(
                time = as.numeric(dynamic_data$term),
                estimate = dynamic_data$estimate,
                conf.low = dynamic_data$conf.low,
                conf.high = dynamic_data$conf.high,
                is_significant = (dynamic_data$conf.low > 0) | (dynamic_data$conf.high < 0),
                outcome = outcome_vars[i],
                outcome_label = outcome_names[i]
            )
            event_study_data[[length(event_study_data) + 1]] <- df
        }
    }
}

# Combine all data
plot_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(event_study_data)

# Filter to exclude month 7 and remove NA outcomes
plot_data <- plot_data[plot_data$time >= -6 & plot_data$time <= 6, ]
plot_data <- plot_data[!is.na(plot_data$outcome_label), ]

# Enforce facet order to match outcome_vars
plot_data$outcome_label <- factor(plot_data$outcome_label, levels = outcome_names)

# Create single faceted plot
event_study_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = time, y = estimate)) +
    # Significant points (filled)
    geom_point(data = subset(plot_data, is_significant),
               size = 1.5, shape = 19) +
    # Non-significant points (hollow)
    geom_point(data = subset(plot_data, !is_significant),
               size = 1.5, shape = 21, fill = "transparent") +
    # Error bars for significant effects (solid)
    geom_errorbar(data = subset(plot_data, is_significant),
                  aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
                  width = 0.5, linetype = "solid") +
    # Error bars for non-significant effects (dotted)
    geom_errorbar(data = subset(plot_data, !is_significant),
                  aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
                  width = 0.5, linetype = "dotted") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = -0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = -6:6) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-6.5, 6.5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.05))) +
    labs(
        x = "Months Relative to Cursor Adoption",
        y = "Treatment Effect",
        caption = "Filled dots: p < 0.05 (significant), Hollow dots: p ≥ 0.05 (non-significant)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, margin = margin(t = 3))
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ outcome_label, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1)

print(event_study_plot)

ggsave("../plots/dynamic_effects_borusyak.pdf",
  plot = event_study_plot, width = 11, height = 2.5,
  device = CairoPDF, family = "DejaVu Sans")
```

## Summary Statistics for All Settings

```{r summary_stats_table}
# Helper function to filter treatment repos by condition and get matched controls
filter_with_matched_controls <- function(dt, treat_condition) {
    treat_subset <- dt[dt$treated == 1 & treat_condition, ]
    treat_repo_names <- unique(treat_subset$repo_name_str)
    matched_controls <- get_matched_controls(treat_repo_names)
    dt[(dt$treated == 1 & dt$repo_name_str %in% treat_repo_names) |
       (dt$treated == 0 & dt$repo_name_str %in% matched_controls), ]
}

# Build filtered variants directly from the already prepared `panel_data`
build_setting_variant <- function(data, setting_label) {
    dt <- data
    if (setting_label == "Orginal") {
        # No filtering
    } else if (setting_label == "Active Months (>0 commits)") {
        dt <- dt[dt$commits > 0, ]
    } else if (setting_label == "Very Active (>=10 commits)") {
        dt <- dt[dt$commits >= log(11), ]
    } else if (setting_label == "Truncated to 2025-06") {
        dt <- dt[dt$time <= 202506, ]
    } else if (setting_label == "High Adoption (>=80%)") {
        adoption_vec <- adoption_pct[dt$repo_name_str]
        adoption_vec[is.na(adoption_vec)] <- 0
        dt <- filter_with_matched_controls(dt, adoption_vec >= 80)
    } else if (setting_label == "High Confidence") {
        dt <- dt[dt$high_confidence == TRUE | is.na(dt$high_confidence), ]
    } else if (setting_label == "Low Confidence") {
        dt <- dt[dt$high_confidence == FALSE | is.na(dt$high_confidence), ]
    } else if (setting_label == "Cursor and Others") {
        dt <- dt[dt$other_agents == TRUE | is.na(dt$other_agents), ]
    } else if (setting_label == "Only Cursor") {
        dt <- dt[dt$other_agents == FALSE | is.na(dt$other_agents), ]
    } else if (setting_label == "JS/TS") {
        dt <- filter_with_matched_controls(dt, dt$lang_group == "JS/TS")
    } else if (setting_label == "Python") {
        dt <- filter_with_matched_controls(dt, dt$lang_group == "Python")
    } else if (setting_label == "Go") {
        dt <- filter_with_matched_controls(dt, dt$lang_group == "Go")
    } else if (setting_label == "Rust") {
        dt <- filter_with_matched_controls(dt, dt$lang_group == "Rust")
    }
    dt
}

# Comprehensive function to calculate statistics for a setting
calculate_setting_stats <- function(setting_name, data) {
    data_s <- build_setting_variant(data, setting_name)

    # Count unique treatment and control repos
    n_treat <- length(unique(data_s$repo_name[data_s$event > 0]))
    n_control <- length(unique(data_s$repo_name[data_s$event == 0]))

    # Total observations
    n_obs <- nrow(data_s)

    # Post-treatment observations (time >= event for treated units)
    post_treat_obs <- sum(data_s$treated == 1 & data_s$time >= data_s$event)

    data.frame(
        Setting = setting_name,
        Treatment_Repos = n_treat,
        Control_Repos = n_control,
        Total_Observations = n_obs,
        Post_Treatment_Obs = post_treat_obs,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
}

# All settings from the notebook
all_settings <- c(
    "Orginal",
    "High Adoption (>=80%)",
    "Active Months (>0 commits)",
    "Very Active (>=10 commits)",
    "High Confidence",
    "Low Confidence",
    "Cursor and Others",
    "Only Cursor",
    "Truncated to 2025-06",
    "JS/TS",
    "Python",
    "Go",
    "Rust"
)

# Calculate stats for all settings
stats_list <- lapply(all_settings, calculate_setting_stats, data = panel_data)
stats_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(stats_list)

# Format the table nicely
knitr::kable(stats_df,
    col.names = c("Setting", "# Treatment Repos", "# Control Repos",
                  "# Total Observations", "# Post-Treatment Obs"),
    format = "html",
    caption = "Summary Statistics for All Analysis Settings",
    align = c("l", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
    format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
) %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(
        bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
        full_width = FALSE,
        position = "left"
    ) %>%
    kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
    kableExtra::pack_rows("Main Settings", 1, 8) %>%
    kableExtra::pack_rows("Language-Specific Settings", 9, 12)
```

## Compare Event Studies Across Settings

```{r compare_event_study, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
settings <- c(
    "Orginal",
    "High Adoption (>=80%)",
    "Active Months (>0 commits)",
    "Very Active (>=10 commits)",
    # "Truncated to 2025-06",
    "High Confidence",
    "Low Confidence",
    "Cursor and Others",
    "Only Cursor"
)

# Run dynamic DiD for each outcome and each setting, collect results
collect_dynamic_results <- function(outcomes, settings) {
    results <- list()
    for (s in settings) {
        data_s <- build_setting_variant(panel_data, s)
        for (y in outcomes) {
            dyn <- didimputation::did_imputation(
                data = data_s,
                yname = y,
                gname = "event",
                tname = "time",
                idname = "repo_name",
                first_stage = ~ age + ncloc + contributors + stars + issues | repo_name + time,
                horizon = -6:6,
                pretrends = -6:-2
            )
            dyn$setting <- s
            dyn$outcome <- y
            results[[length(results) + 1]] <- dyn
        }
    }
    dplyr::bind_rows(results)
}

dyn_all <- collect_dynamic_results(outcome_vars, settings)

# Count treatment and control repos for each setting
count_setting_stats <- function(setting_name) {
    data_s <- build_setting_variant(panel_data, setting_name)
    n_treat <- length(unique(data_s$repo_name[data_s$event > 0]))
    n_control <- length(unique(data_s$repo_name[data_s$event == 0]))
    n_obs <- nrow(data_s)
    list(treat = n_treat, control = n_control, obs = n_obs)
}
setting_stats <- lapply(settings, count_setting_stats)
settings_with_counts <- paste0(settings, " (n=", sapply(setting_stats, `[[`, "treat"),
                               "+", sapply(setting_stats, `[[`, "control"),
                               ", obs=", sapply(setting_stats, `[[`, "obs"), ")")
names(settings_with_counts) <- settings

plot_dyn <- dyn_all[dyn_all$term != "treat", ]
plot_dyn$time <- as.numeric(plot_dyn$term)

plot_dyn <- plot_dyn[plot_dyn$time >= -6 & plot_dyn$time <= 6, ]

label_map <- setNames(outcome_names, outcome_vars)
plot_dyn$outcome_label <- label_map[plot_dyn$outcome]
# Remove rows with NA outcome_label (outcomes not in our list)
plot_dyn <- plot_dyn[!is.na(plot_dyn$outcome_label), ]
plot_dyn$outcome_label <- factor(plot_dyn$outcome_label, levels = outcome_names)
# Update setting labels with counts and enforce order
plot_dyn$setting_label <- settings_with_counts[plot_dyn$setting]
plot_dyn$setting_label <- factor(plot_dyn$setting_label, levels = settings_with_counts, ordered = TRUE)
# Also keep original setting as ordered factor for consistent dodging
plot_dyn$setting <- factor(plot_dyn$setting, levels = settings, ordered = TRUE)
plot_dyn$significant <- (plot_dyn$conf.low > 0) | (plot_dyn$conf.high < 0)

# Add columns for aesthetics
plot_dyn$linetype_sig <- ifelse(plot_dyn$significant, "solid", "dotted")
plot_dyn$shape_sig <- ifelse(plot_dyn$significant, 19, 21)  # filled vs hollow

# Create shared position dodge
pos <- position_dodge(width = 0.5)

# Overlayed event study by setting, faceted by outcome
dynamic_plot <- ggplot(plot_dyn, aes(x = time, y = estimate, color = setting_label)) +
    # Error bars (single layer with linetype mapped to significance)
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high, linetype = linetype_sig),
                  position = pos, width = 0.4) +
    # Points (single layer with shape mapped to significance)
    geom_point(aes(shape = significant, fill = after_scale(ifelse(significant, color, NA))),
               position = pos, size = 1.5) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 19, "FALSE" = 21), guide = "none") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = -0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = -6:6) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-6.5, 6.5)) +
    scale_linetype_identity() +
    scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c(
        "#000000",  # Orginal - Black
        "#E69F00",  # High Adoption - Orange
        "#56B4E9",  # Active Months - Sky blue
        "#009E73",  # Very Active - Green
        "#0072B2",  # High Confidence - Blue
        "#D55E00",  # Low Confidence - Vermillion
        "#CC79A7",  # Cursor and Others - Pink
        "#999999"   # Only Cursor - Grey
    ), settings_with_counts), drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
        x = "Months Relative to Cursor Adoption",
        y = "Treatment Effect",
        color = "Setting",
        caption = "Filled dots: p < 0.05 (significant), Hollow dots: p ≥ 0.05 (non-significant)"
    ) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 4)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, margin = margin(t = 3)),
        legend.margin = margin(b = 3),
        legend.box.margin = margin(t = -5)
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ outcome_label, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1)

print(dynamic_plot)

ggsave("../plots/dynamic_effects_comparison.pdf",
  plot = dynamic_plot, width = 10, height = 10,
  device = CairoPDF, family = "DejaVu Sans")
```

## Compare Event Studies Across Programming Languages

```{r compare_languages, fig.width=8, fig.height=10}
lang_settings <- c("Orginal", "JS/TS", "Python", "Go", "Rust")

lang_dyn_all <- collect_dynamic_results(outcome_vars, lang_settings)

# Count treatment and control repos for each language setting
count_lang_stats <- function(setting_name) {
    data_s <- build_setting_variant(panel_data, setting_name)
    n_treat <- length(unique(data_s$repo_name[data_s$event > 0]))
    n_control <- length(unique(data_s$repo_name[data_s$event == 0]))
    n_obs <- nrow(data_s)
    list(treat = n_treat, control = n_control, obs = n_obs)
}
lang_stats <- lapply(lang_settings, count_lang_stats)
lang_settings_with_counts <- paste0(lang_settings, " (n=", sapply(lang_stats, `[[`, "treat"),
                                    "+", sapply(lang_stats, `[[`, "control"),
                                    ", obs=", sapply(lang_stats, `[[`, "obs"), ")")
names(lang_settings_with_counts) <- lang_settings

plot_lang <- lang_dyn_all[lang_dyn_all$term != "treat", ]
plot_lang$time <- as.numeric(plot_lang$term)
plot_lang <- plot_lang[plot_lang$time >= -6 & plot_lang$time <= 6, ]

plot_lang$outcome_label <- label_map[plot_lang$outcome]
# Remove rows with NA outcome_label (outcomes not in our list)
plot_lang <- plot_lang[!is.na(plot_lang$outcome_label), ]
plot_lang$outcome_label <- factor(plot_lang$outcome_label, levels = outcome_names)

# Update setting labels with counts and enforce order
plot_lang$setting_label <- lang_settings_with_counts[plot_lang$setting]
plot_lang$setting_label <- factor(plot_lang$setting_label, levels = lang_settings_with_counts, ordered = TRUE)
# Also keep original setting as ordered factor for consistent dodging
plot_lang$setting <- factor(plot_lang$setting, levels = lang_settings, ordered = TRUE)
plot_lang$significant <- (plot_lang$conf.low > 0) | (plot_lang$conf.high < 0)
plot_lang$linetype_sig <- ifelse(plot_lang$significant, "solid", "dotted")

# Create shared position dodge
pos_lang <- position_dodge(width = 0.5)

# Create language comparison plot
lang_plot <- ggplot(plot_lang, aes(x = time, y = estimate, color = setting_label)) +
    # Error bars (single layer with linetype mapped to significance)
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high, linetype = linetype_sig),
                  position = pos_lang, width = 0.4) +
    # Points (single layer with shape mapped to significance)
    geom_point(aes(shape = significant, fill = after_scale(ifelse(significant, color, NA))),
               position = pos_lang, size = 1.5) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 19, "FALSE" = 21), guide = "none") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = -0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = -6:6) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-6.5, 6.5)) +
    scale_linetype_identity() +
    scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c(
        "#000000",  # Orginal - Black
        "#E69F00",  # JS/TS - Orange
        "#0072B2",  # Python - Blue
        "#009E73",  # Go - Green
        "#CC79A7"   # Rust - Pink
    ), lang_settings_with_counts), drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
        x = "Months Relative to Cursor Adoption",
        y = "Treatment Effect",
        color = "Language Group",
        caption = "Filled dots: p < 0.05 (significant), Hollow dots: p ≥ 0.05 (non-significant)"
    ) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, margin = margin(t = 3)),
        legend.margin = margin(b = 3),
        legend.box.margin = margin(t = -5)
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ outcome_label, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1)

print(lang_plot)

ggsave("../plots/dynamic_effects_languages.pdf",
  plot = lang_plot, width = 8, height = 10,
  device = CairoPDF, family = "DejaVu Sans")
```
